keyPoints: Introduction: Map Reduce is a programming model for processing large data sets in parallel across a cluster of nodes.
Mapper: Mapping is the first stage of the Map Reduce process where each input record is split into a key-value pair and mapped to a key.
Reducer: Reducing is the final stage of the Map Reduce process where the mapped key-value pairs are aggregated to produce the final output.
Combiner: Combiner is a component that combines the mapped key-value pairs and reduces the data size before it is sent to the reducer.
Partitioner: Partitioner is a component that divides the mapped key-value pairs into partitions based on a specific key.
Searching: Map Reduce can be used for searching large data sets by using a mapper to find the location of the data and a reducer to retrieve the data.
Sorting: Map Reduce can be used for sorting large data sets by using a mapper to sort the data and a reducer to combine the sorted data.
Compression: Map Reduce can be used for compressing large data sets by using a mapper to compress the data and a reducer to combine the compressed data.
